form_semantics.justification: Every input has a visible <label>. All three steps use <fieldset>/<legend>. Email field correctly uses aria-invalid='true' and aria-describedby referencing both the hint and the error paragraph. Error message is actionable ('Enter a valid email address, like name@example.com.'). Correct input types (email, tel, text), full autocomplete attributes on all fields including cc-number/cc-exp/cc-csc. Required fields indicated with asterisk + explanatory note and the native required attribute — not colour-only.

heading_hierarchy.justification: Single <h1> for the site name in <header>, <h2> for 'Checkout' in <main>. Step titles are <legend> elements inside fieldsets — the correct choice for form group labels, not heading elements — so there are no skipped or misused heading levels.

element_choice.justification: All interactive controls are native elements (<button>, <input>, <select>). Progress indicator uses <nav>/<ol>/<li>. Order summary uses a fully-structured <table> with <caption>, <thead>, <tbody>, <tfoot>. No div or span used in place of a semantic element.

aria_discipline.justification:
ARIA is used appropriately throughout: aria-current='step' on the active progress item, aria-label on the
, aria-invalid/aria-describedby on the error field, aria-hidden on decorative icons and redundant visible labels. Minor issue: the
carries aria-label='Checkout' when the adjacent

Checkout

could be referenced via aria-labelledby, making the aria-label slightly redundant rather than additive.

content_realism.justification: Content is fully realistic: real person name (Margaret Okonkwo), plausible email and address placeholders, named products with variants (colour, size), specific prices that sum correctly ($158.00 + $94.50 = $252.50). No 'Product 1' or Lorem ipsum anywhere.

landmark_structure.justification: Skip link present and targets <main>. <header>, <main>, and <nav> (with aria-label) used correctly. <main> wraps only page-specific content. No spurious section or landmark overuse.

list_semantics.justification: Progress steps are in an <ol> (ordered, since sequence matters). No lists used gratuitously.

table_semantics.justification: <table> used for genuine two-dimensional tabular order data with <caption>, <thead>/<tbody>/<tfoot>, and <th scope='col'> on all column headers. Not used for layout.

expectations_met: Each step uses <fieldset> with <legend> for grouping
All inputs have associated <label> elements (not placeholder-only)
Email error uses aria-invalid='true' and aria-describedby pointing to error message
Error message text is actionable — states problem and gives a concrete example fix
Progress indicator conveys current step accessibly via aria-current='step' and descriptive aria-labels on step bubbles
Card number, expiry, CVV fields use autocomplete='cc-number', 'cc-exp', 'cc-csc'
Order summary uses a <table> with full semantic structure
Submit and Back buttons are <button> elements with clear labels
No placeholder text used as a substitute for labels
Steps use <legend> rather than all-h1 or all-h2; page heading hierarchy is h1 → h2
Required fields indicated with asterisk + explanatory note + native required attribute
additional_observations: aria-label='Checkout' on the <form> works, but aria-labelledby pointing to the <h2> would be marginally preferable to avoid redundancy

The required-note paragraph hides the asterisk from screen readers via aria-hidden, but the native required attribute on each input still conveys required status to assistive technology — net result is correct
role='alert' on the error paragraph is a good addition for dynamic injection scenarios
